questions.text: The units of electric intensity are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The two units of electric intensity are volts per meter (V/m) and newtons per coulomb (N/C). The electric field strength (intensity) is measured in volts per meter (V/m). It is a measure of the electric force acting on a charged particle per unit charge. The electric current is measured in amperes (A) or coulombs per second (C/s), as mentioned earlier. The electric charge is measured in coulombs (C). The electric potential difference (voltage) is measured in volts (V). The electric field strength and electric potential difference are related by the equation E = V/d, where E is the electric field strength, V is the voltage, and d is the distance between the charged particles. The electric force acting on a charged particle is measured in newtons (N). The electric field strength and electric force are related by the equation F = Eq, where F is the electric force, E is the electric field strength/intensity, and q is the charge of the particle with the unit coulomb.

Volt/meter is a unit of electric intensity but meter/coulomb is not. Meter/coulomb is a unit of electric potential, also known as electric potential difference or voltage. It is measured in volts (V). Electric potential is a scalar quantity that describes the amount of electric potential energy per unit charge at a given point in space. It is often used to describe the difference in electric potential between two points in an electric field. The electric potential difference between two points is measured in volts and is equal to the work required to move a unit charge from one point to the other.

Newton/coulomb is a unit of electric intensity but volt/coulomb is not. Volt/coulomb is a unit of electric potential, also known as electric potential difference or voltage. It is measured in volts (V). Electric potential is a scalar quantity that describes the amount of electric potential energy per unit charge at a given point in space. It is often used to describe the difference in electric potential between two points in an electric field. The electric potential difference between two points is measured in volts and is equal to the work required to move a unit charge from one point to the other.
Newton/coulomb is a unit of electric intensity but joule/meter is not. Joule per meter is a unit of energy per unit length, also known as energy density.

questions.explanation: The two units of electric intensity are volts per meter (V/m) and newtons per coulomb (N/C). The electric field strength (intensity) is measured in volts per meter (V/m). It is a measure of the electric force acting on a charged particle per unit charge. The electric current is measured in amperes (A) or coulombs per second (C/s), as mentioned earlier. The electric charge is measured in coulombs (C). The electric potential difference (voltage) is measured in volts (V). The electric field strength and electric potential difference are related by the equation E = V/d, where E is the electric field strength, V is the voltage, and d is the distance between the charged particles. The electric force acting on a charged particle is measured in newtons (N). The electric field strength and electric force are related by the equation F = Eq, where F is the electric force, E is the electric field strength/intensity, and q is the charge of the particle with the unit coulomb.

questions.text: Farad is defined as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Newton per volt is not a unit of capacitance. It is the SI unit of electric field strength, also known as voltage gradient or electric field gradient. The electric field strength is defined as the force per unit charge experienced by a small test charge placed in an electric field. The unit of electric field strength is newton per coulomb (N/C), but it can also be expressed as volt per meter (V/m) because the volt is equivalent to a joule per coulomb (J/C).

A farad is the SI unit of capacitance. It is defined as the amount of electric charge that can be stored per unit of voltage in a capacitor. One farad is equal to one coulomb of electric charge per volt of potential difference. So, it's Coulomb/volt.

Coulomb per joule is the SI unit of electric potential difference, also known as voltage. The electric potential difference between two points in an electric field is defined as the work done per unit charge in moving a positive test charge from one point to the other. The unit of electric potential difference is the volt (V), which is equivalent to one joule of energy per coulomb of charge.

Coulomb per newton is the SI unit of electric charge to mass ratio, also known as a specific charge. It is defined as the electric charge of a particle divided by its mass. The unit of specific charge is coulomb per kilogram (C/kg) or coulomb per gram (C/g), but it can also be expressed as newton to tesla (N/T) because the tesla is equivalent to a newton per ampere per meter (N/A.m). The specific charge is a fundamental property of a charged particle and is used to identify and study various types of particles, such as electrons, protons, and ions.

questions.explanation: A farad is the SI unit of capacitance. It is defined as the amount of electric charge that can be stored per unit of voltage in a capacitor. One farad is equal to one coulomb of electric charge per volt of potential difference. So, it's Coulomb/volt.

questions.text: A charge is moving with velocity v, it enters a uniform electric field E. The direction of v and E are not parallel. What is the path of the charged particle inside the electric field?

options.explanation: When a charged particle enters a uniform electric field at an angle to the field lines, it experiences a force perpendicular to its velocity. This force causes the particle's path to bend, resulting in a parabolic trajectory.

A circular path would occur if the charge were moving perpendicular to the electric field and experienced a force directed toward the center of the circular path. However, in this case, the direction of the velocity and electric field are not necessarily perpendicular, so the path is not circular.

If the path were parallel to the velocity (v), it would mean that the charge is not experiencing any force due to the electric field. However, since the charge enters the electric field, it will experience a force and change its path.

If the path is parallel to E, it would mean that the charged particle is moving along the electric field lines without any deviation. This would only happen if the initial velocity of the charged particle is parallel to the electric field, in which case the electric force would not affect the direction of motion.

questions.text: The study of electric charges at rest in an electric field is known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electromagnetism is a branch of physics that deals with the study of electric and magnetic phenomena, including their interactions with each other. While electromagnetism encompasses a broader range of topics beyond just electric charges at rest, it includes phenomena such as electromagnetic induction, Maxwell's equations, and electromagnetic waves. Therefore, while electromagnetism includes electrostatics as a subset, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

Electrostatics is the branch of physics concerned with the study of stationary electric charges and the electric fields they create. It focuses on understanding the behavior of electric charges when they are at rest and the interactions between them. Topics in electrostatics include Coulomb's law, electric fields, electric potential, capacitance, and the behavior of conductors and insulators. Therefore, electrostatics is specifically dedicated to the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

Quantum physics, also known as quantum mechanics, is a branch of physics that deals with phenomena at the smallest scales, typically at the level of atoms, molecules, and subatomic particles. While quantum physics encompasses a wide range of topics, including the behavior of particles with electric charge, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field. Instead, it addresses phenomena such as wave-particle duality, quantum superposition, and quantum entanglement.

Magnetism is the branch of physics that deals with the study of magnetic fields, magnetic forces, and magnetic materials. While magnetism is closely related to electricity, particularly through the electromagnetic force described by Maxwell's equations, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field. Instead, magnetism primarily concerns the behavior of magnetic fields and magnetic materials, including phenomena such as magnetic induction, magnetic dipoles, and magnetic domains.

questions.explanation: Electrostatics is the branch of physics concerned with the study of stationary electric charges and the electric fields they create. It focuses on understanding the behavior of electric charges when they are at rest and the interactions between them. Topics in electrostatics include Coulomb's law, electric fields, electric potential, capacitance, and the behavior of conductors and insulators. Therefore, electrostatics is specifically dedicated to the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

questions.text: You have two identical capacitors. They can be connected in series or parallel. If you want the smallest equivalent capacitance for the combination, how should you connect them?

In series, the net capacitance of the capacitors is less than the individual capacitances. Ct = 1/C1+1/C2 + 1/C3Thus any values for C1, C2, and C3 will become less than the original as they are each divided by 1.

Aluminum is not a semiconductor. Semiconductors have properties that fall between those of conductors and insulators, and they can exhibit variable conductivity depending on external conditions. Aluminum, on the other hand, is a metal and is classified as a conductor due to its high electrical conductivity.

Aluminium is not an insulator. Insulators are materials that have very low conductivity and do not allow the flow of electric current. Aluminium, as mentioned earlier, is an excellent conductor and allows the flow of electric current.

Aluminium is not a photoconductor. Photoconductors are materials that experience changes in their electrical conductivity when exposed to light. While some materials, such as certain types of silicon, can be used as photoconductors, aluminium does not exhibit this property.

questions.text: The time constant is defined as the time required by the capacitor _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: To deposit 63% of the equilibrium charge
options.explanation: In a charging or discharging capacitor-resistor circuit, the time constant (τ) is defined as the time it takes for the capacitor voltage (or charge) to reach approximately 63.2% of its final (equilibrium) value. This value corresponds to approximately 1 - 1/e, where e is Euler's number (approximately 2.71828). This definition is widely used in electronics and circuit analysis. Therefore, option a) To deposit 63% of the equilibrium charge, is the correct choice for defining the time constant of a capacitor.

options.text: To deposit 36% of the equilibrium charge
options.explanation: This option suggests that the time constant is the time required for the capacitor to reach 36% of its equilibrium charge. However, this is not consistent with the standard definition of the time constant. In capacitor charging or discharging circuits, the time constant corresponds to approximately 63.2% of the equilibrium charge, not 36%. Therefore, option b is incorrect.

options.text: To deposit 63 times of the equilibrium charge
options.explanation: This option suggests that the time constant is related to depositing 63 times the equilibrium charge, which is not accurate. The time constant is a measure of time, not a multiplier of charge. It is defined as the time it takes for the capacitor voltage (or charge) to reach approximately 63.2% of its final (equilibrium) value, not 63 times the equilibrium charge. Therefore, option c is incorrect.

options.text: To deposit 36 times of the equilibrium charge

Instead, it represents the time it takes for the capacitor voltage (or charge) to reach approximately 63.2% of its final (equilibrium) value. Therefore, option d is incorrect.

questions.text: The study of electric charges at rest in an electric field is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electromagnetism is a branch of physics that deals with the study of electric and magnetic phenomena, including their interactions with each other. While electromagnetism encompasses a broader range of topics beyond just electric charges at rest, it includes phenomena such as electromagnetic induction, Maxwell's equations, and electromagnetic waves. Therefore, while electromagnetism includes electrostatics as a subset, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

Electrostatics is the branch of physics concerned with the study of stationary electric charges and the electric fields they create. It focuses on understanding the behavior of electric charges when they are at rest and the interactions between them. Topics in electrostatics include Coulomb's law, electric fields, electric potential, capacitance, and the behavior of conductors and insulators. Therefore, electrostatics is specifically dedicated to the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

Quantum physics, also known as quantum mechanics, is a branch of physics that deals with phenomena at the smallest scales, typically at the level of atoms, molecules, and subatomic particles. While quantum physics encompasses a wide range of topics, including the behavior of particles with electric charge, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field. Instead, it addresses phenomena such as wave-particle duality, quantum superposition, and quantum entanglement.

Magnetism is the branch of physics that deals with the study of magnetic fields, magnetic forces, and magnetic materials. While magnetism is closely related to electricity, particularly through the electromagnetic force described by Maxwell's equations, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field. Instead, magnetism primarily concerns the behavior of magnetic fields and magnetic materials, including phenomena such as magnetic induction, magnetic dipoles, and magnetic domains.

questions.explanation: Electrostatics is the branch of physics concerned with the study of stationary electric charges and the electric fields they create. It focuses on understanding the behavior of electric charges when they are at rest and the interactions between them. Topics in electrostatics include Coulomb's law, electric fields, electric potential, capacitance, and the behavior of conductors and insulators. Therefore, electrostatics is specifically dedicated to the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

questions.text: What is a capacitor?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A device that produces audio sounds
options.explanation: This option describes a speaker or a transducer, which converts electrical signals into sound waves. It does not accurately describe a capacitor.

options.text: A device that dissipates electric charge
options.explanation: This option describes a resistor or a device that converts electrical energy into heat or other forms of energy. It does not accurately describe a capacitor.

options.text: A device that stores electric charge in an electric field
options.explanation: A capacitor is an electronic component used in electrical circuits to store and release electrical energy. It consists of two conductive plates separated by an insulating material called a dielectric. When a voltage difference (potential difference) is applied across the plates, electric charge accumulates on the plates, creating an electric field between them. This stored electric charge in the electric field creates potential energy, which can be released when needed. Capacitors are commonly used in various electronic devices and circuits for energy storage, filtering, timing, and coupling applications.

options.text: A device that emits radio signals
options.explanation: This option describes a radio transmitter or an antenna, which emits electromagnetic waves for communication purposes. It does not accurately describe a capacitor.

questions.explanation: A capacitor is a device that stores electric charges in an electric field. A capacitor is an electrical component that stores potential energy. Capacitors hold positive and negative energy on two separate plates separated by an insulator. A capacitor(s) is called cap(s) for short.

questions.text: If there is a frictional force between two surfaces, then which of the following will be created?

options.explanation: Friction between two surfaces can lead to the separation of charges, resulting in the creation of an electric field. This occurs due to the transfer of electrons from one surface to another, creating a charge imbalance.

When two surfaces experience friction, charge separation occurs, leading to the generation of static electricity. This is the correct answer as it directly results from the frictional force between the surfaces.

Friction between surfaces does not directly produce a magnetic field. While magnetic fields can arise from moving charges, friction does not involve such movement in a way that would create a magnetic field.

This option is incorrect because, while friction can create an electric field, it does not directly lead to the generation of a magnetic field. Therefore, it cannot be true that all these effects occur due to friction.

questions.explanation: Friction between two surfaces causes charge separation as electrons are transferred from one material to another. This results in one surface becoming positively charged and the other negatively charged, thus creating an electric field between them. The process does not involve the creation of new charges, as they are simply redistributed from one surface to the other. Option A is incorrect because while friction creates charge, it does not directly create an electric field in the traditional sense. Option C is incorrect because there is no direct relationship between friction and the generation of a magnetic field. Option D is also incorrect since it includes the creation of a magnetic field, which does not occur due to friction.

questions.text: What effect does a dielectric have on the capacitance of a capacitor?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a dielectric material is inserted between the plates of a capacitor, it increases the capacitance of the capacitor. The extent of this increase is determined by the dielectric constant (k) or relative permittivity of the material. The dielectric constant represents the ratio of the capacitance of the capacitor with the dielectric material to the capacitance of the same capacitor without the dielectric material. Mathematically, the capacitance with the dielectric (C' ) is given by C'=k×C, where C is the capacitance without the dielectric. This formula indicates that the capacitance increases by a factor of k when a dielectric material is present. Therefore, option A) Increases by k accurately describes the effect of a dielectric on the capacitance.

This option suggests that the presence of a dielectric material has no effect on the capacitance of the capacitor. However, this is not accurate. The presence of a dielectric material between the plates of a capacitor increases the capacitance, as explained in option A). Therefore, option B) Has no effect is incorrect.

This option suggests that the capacitance of the capacitor decreases by a factor of k when a dielectric material is inserted. However, this is not the case. The presence of a dielectric material increases the capacitance by a factor of k, as explained in option A). Therefore, option C) Decreases by k is incorrect.

This option suggests that the capacitance of the capacitor increases by half of the dielectric constant (k) when a dielectric material is inserted. However, this is not accurate. The presence of a dielectric material increases the capacitance by the full value of the dielectric constant (k), not by half of it. Therefore, option D) Increases by k/2 is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Introducing a dielectric in a capacitor causes a decrease in the electric field, and hence the voltage. The charge stored on the plate remains unchanged.As C = Q/V,decreasing V causes an increase in capacitance.The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is given by:capacitance C = (area of plate) (permittivity of free space) / (distance between the plates) = Aεo/d ..........(1)Introducing the dielectric, the capacitance becomes:capacitance Cm = (area of plate) (permittivity of medium) / (distance between the plates) = Aεm/d .......... (2)The relative permittivity is given by:k = εm/εoor εm = kεoPutting (εm = kεo ) in equation 2:Cm = Akεo/d = Aεo/d x kCm = C x kHence, the introduction of dielectric causes the capacitance to increase by k times.

questions.text: What is the magnitude of a point charge which produces an electric field of 2 N/C at a distance of 60 cm?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is correct as using the formula E = k * |q| / r2 and substituting the given values, the magnitude of the charge is calculated to be 8 × 10−11C.
This option incorrectly suggests the magnitude of the charge as 2 × 10−12C. Recalculate using the formula to see the correct result.
This option incorrectly suggests the magnitude of the charge as 3 × 10−11C. The correct calculation yields a different result.
This option overestimates the charge magnitude as 6 × 10−10C. Follow the correct formula to find the accurate value.

questions.explanation: The electric field (E) due to a point charge (q) at a distance (r) is given by the formula E = k * |q| / r2, where k is the electrostatic constant (approximately 8.99 × 109 N m2/C2). Here, E = 2 N/C and r = 60 cm = 0.6 m. Rearranging the formula to solve for |q|, we have |q| = E * r2 / k. Substituting the values, |q| = (2 N/C) * (0.6 m)2 / (8.99 × 109 N m2/C2) = 8 × 10−11C. Therefore, the correct answer is 8 × 10−11C. The other options fail to provide the correct magnitude as they either miscalculate or misinterpret the formula and given values.

questions.text: In a uniform electric field, which statement is correct?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: All charged particles experience the same force
options.explanation: In a uniform electric field, the force experienced by a charged particle depends on the charge of the particle (q) and the electric field strength (E). The force (F) is calculated using F = qE, meaning particles with different charges experience different forces. Thus, this option is incorrect.

options.text: All charged particles move with the same velocity
options.explanation: Charged particles in a uniform electric field experience acceleration proportional to their charge-to-mass ratio (q/m), but this does not guarantee the same velocity. Initial velocity, exposure time, and other forces affect their actual velocities. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

options.text: All electric field lines are directed towards positive charges
options.explanation: Electric field lines originate from positive charges and terminate at negative charges. In a uniform field, lines are straight and parallel, not necessarily directed towards positive charges. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

options.text: All electric field lines are parallel
options.explanation: In a uniform electric field, electric field lines are parallel, straight, and evenly spaced, indicating uniform strength and direction. Therefore, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: In case of the uniform electric field, the two electric field lines are1. straight2. parallel3. equidistantA uniform electric field is a field in which the value of the field strength remains the same

questions.text: The electric field strength between a pair of plates is "E". If the separation of the plates is doubled and the potential difference between the plates is increased by a factor of four, the new field strength is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A suggests that the electric field strength remains unchanged. However, this is incorrect. When the separation of the plates is doubled and the potential difference between the plates is increased by a factor of four, the electric field strength is not expected to remain the same. Therefore, option A is incorrect.

Option B correctly indicates that the new electric field strength is twice the original electric field strength. Doubling the separation of the plates while quadrupling the potential difference between them results in an electric field strength that is proportional to 4V/2d=2E. Therefore, option B is the correct choice.

Option C suggests that the new electric field strength is four times the original electric field strength. However, this is not the case. Doubling the separation of the plates and quadrupling the potential difference between them does not result in an electric field strength that is directly proportional to 4V/2d=2E. Therefore, option C is incorrect.

Option D suggests that the new electric field strength is eight times the original electric field strength. However, this is not the case. Doubling the separation of the plates and quadrupling the potential difference between them does not result in an electric field strength that is directly proportional to 4V/2d =2E. Therefore, option D is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Electric field is calculated by the formula = Voltage / Distance so doubling the separation of plates decreases the electric field by 2 times and increasing pd between plates by factor of four, increases electric field 4 times so the over all increase is 2E
hints: increased by a factor of four

questions.text: What happens when a charge is placed on a soap bubble?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a charge is placed on a soap bubble, it does not collapse immediately due to the charge. The surface tension of the soap bubble, which is responsible for holding it together, is not significantly affected by the charge. The charges on the soap bubble are distributed on the curved surface, and the repulsive forces between like charges try to spread them as far apart as possible, leading to an even distribution.

When some charge is placed on a soap bubble then the radius of the bubble will increase. Firstly, the charge will be distributed uniformly on the surface of a bubble. Secondly, the bubble will expand because the charged particles uniformly distributed on it cause them to repel each other due to the electrostatic force.

When a charge is placed on a soap bubble, the charges repel each other, causing the bubble to expand slightly (as explained in option b). However, this slight expansion in the radius might be followed by a decrease in the radius if the electrostatic forces are strong enough to overcome the surface tension. In this case, the bubble might collapse or burst, leading to a decrease in its radius.
This option is incorrect since option B is the correct choice.

questions.explanation: When some charge is placed on a soap bubble then the radius of the bubble will increase. Firstly, the charge will be distributed uniformly on the surface of a bubble. Secondly, the bubble will expand because the charged particles uniformly distributed on it cause them to repel each other due to the electrostatic force.

questions.text: An electric dipole is situated in a non-uniform electric field. It may experience:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A resultant force and a couple
options.explanation: An electric dipole is defined as a couple of opposite charges “q” and “–q” separated by a distance “d”. By default, the direction of electric dipoles in space is always from negative charge “-q” to positive charge “q”. The midpoint “q” and “–q” is called the center of the dipole. The simplest example of an electric dipole is a pair of electric charges of two opposite signs and equal magnitude separated by distance. Suppose there are two charges of equal magnitude “q”, separated by a distance, “d”. Let the first charge be negative and the second charge is positive. This combination can be called an electric dipole.Electric DipoleAn electric dipole is denoted by the symbol “p”.When electric field is not uniform, the net force will be non-zero, there will also be torque on the system. When dipole moment p is parallel or antiparallel to external field E, the net torque is zero, but there is a force on the dipole E which is not uniform.

questions.text: If a dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor, the net capacitance of the capacitor:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor, the presence of the dielectric reduces the electric field between the plates. This is because the polarized molecules in the dielectric material align themselves with the electric field, weakening it. As a result, the capacitance of the capacitor increases. Therefore, option (a) is not correct.

○The strength of the electric field is reduced due to the presence of dielectric. If the total charge on the plates is kept constant, then the potential difference is reduced across the capacitor plates. In this way, dielectric increases the capacitance of the capacitor.So, option A,C,D is wrong .

If a dielectric material is placed between the plates of a capacitor and the separation distance between the plates remains the same, the capacitance will increase due to the dielectric constant of the material. Therefore, the net capacitance of the capacitor does not remain constant. Hence, option (c) is not correct.

If the dielectric material completely fills the space between the plates of a capacitor, the electric field between the plates is essentially eliminated. In this case, the capacitance of the capacitor becomes zero because there is no potential difference between the plates. However, in most practical scenarios, the dielectric material is not in direct contact with the plates but is inserted between them. Therefore, the option (d) is not correct

If the total charge on the plates is kept constant, then the potential difference is reduced across the capacitor plates. questions.text: Electric Potential which is measured with respect to the zero potential is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Absolute electric potential is the amount of potential energy needed to move a unit positive charge from infinity to a specific point within the field. The electric potential at infinity is assumed to be zero.
Change in kinetic energy is the difference between the final and initial kinetic energy.

Change in gravitational potential energy, ΔP.Eg, is ΔP.Eg = mgh, with h being the increase in height and g the acceleration due to gravity. The gravitational potential energy of an object near the Earth's surface is due to its position in the mass-Earth system.
Electric intensity at a point is the force on a unit positive charge at that point.

questions.text: When a charge gets influenced by its surroundings, it has entered:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: An electric field is the physical field that surrounds electrically charged particles and exerts force on all other charged particles in the field, either attracting or repelling them. Hence, this way influences a charge.
A gravitational field is the force field that exists in the space around every mass or group of masses.

A thermal field, or geothermal field, is the space taken by the installation of a geothermal system. In a geothermal system, wells are dug in the ground at regular intervals. It is the space occupied by these wells that is called a thermal field or a geothermal field.
A closed field is a region with a close surface which starts and stops at the same point.

questions.explanation: The space surrounding a charged object is affected by the presence of the charge; an electric field is established in that space. A charged object creates an electric field - an alteration of the space or field in the region that surrounds it. Other charges in that field would feel the unusual alteration of the space.A charged particle may feel all the other fields but the questions states 'When a charge gets influenced', hence A would be the best option to choose.

questions.text: The electric flux through the surface of the sphere is directly proportional to the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electric flux is directly proportional to the surface area of the sphere. This is because the electric field lines pass through the surface of the sphere and the number of field lines passing through a given area is proportional to that area. As the surface area increases, the number of field lines passing through it also increases, leading to a higher electric flux.this is partially correct.

Electric flux is not directly proportional to the radius of the sphere. While the radius does affect the magnitude of the electric field at different points on the sphere's surface, it is not the primary factor determining the electric flux through the surface.

options.text: Charge at the centre of the sphere
options.explanation: According to Guass' Law flux through a surface is proportional to the charge enclosed at its center.

The electric flux through a closed surface is determined by the net charge enclosed within that surface, regardless of the nature (positive or negative) of the charge. The nature of charge does not directly affect the proportionality of electric flux to any of the given factors.

questions.explanation: According to Guass' Law flux through a surface is proportional to the charge enclosed at its center.

questions.text: Electric Flux through the surface of a sphere which has constant charge at its center depends on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The electric flux through the surface of a sphere is not influenced by the radius the sphere. Gauss's law tells us that the charge enclosed within the surface determines the electric flux.

options.text: The surface area of the sphere
options.explanation: The electric flux through the surface of a sphere is not influenced by the surface area of the sphere. Gauss's law tells us that the charge enclosed within the surface determines the electric flux.

options.text: The amount of charge inside the sphere
options.explanation: As per Gauss's theorem in electrostatics, the electric flux through a surface depends only on the amount of charge enclosed by the surface. It does not depend on size and shape of the surface. Therefore, electric flux through the surface is the same for all figures.

options.text: The amount of charge outside the sphere
options.explanation: The electric flux through the surface of a sphere is not influenced by the amount of charge outside the sphere. Gauss's law tells us that the charge enclosed within the surface determines the electric flux.

questions.text: The flux through a surface will be minimal when the angle between E and △A is:

In this case, the electric field is acting perpendicular to the surface, which results in the smallest component of the electric field vector contributing to the flux through the surface.
The flux through a surface is not necessarily minimum at an angle of 60 degrees. It could be greater or smaller depending on the specific situation.
The flux through a surface is not necessarily minimum at an angle of 30 degrees. It could be greater or smaller depending on the specific situation.
When the angle between E and ∆A is 0 degrees (i.e., they are parallel), the electric flux is maximum. In this case, the entire electric field vector contributes to the flux through the surface.

questions.text: The given diagram shows a capacitive circuit. Which of the following vector diagram shows the current lead in a capacitive circuit?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is correct. In a capacitive circuit, the current leads the voltage by 90 degrees, which is correctly represented in this diagram.
Option B represents an inductive circuit where the voltage leads the current by 90 degrees, which is opposite to what occurs in a capacitive circuit.
Option C does not accurately represent the phase relationship in a capacitive circuit, as it fails to show the correct leading phase of current over voltage.
Option D is incorrect as it also fails to display the proper phase relationship of current leading voltage in a capacitive circuit.

questions.explanation: In a capacitive circuit, the defining characteristic is that the current leads the voltage by 90 degrees. This means that the peak of the current waveform occurs before the peak of the voltage waveform. Option A correctly illustrates this relationship, where the current vector is ahead of the voltage vector by a quarter cycle. Options B, C, and D either show an incorrect phase relationship or a different circuit type, such as an inductive circuit, where the voltage leads the current.

questions.text: Which statement describes the electric potential difference between two points in the electric field of charge Q?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The difference of electric field between the points per unit charge.
options.explanation: This statement describes the electric field, not the electric potential difference. Electric field (E) is the force experienced by a unit positive charge at a point in space. Electric potential difference, on the other hand, refers to the difference in electric potential (voltage) between two points in an electric field. While electric field is measured in units of force per unit charge (N/C), electric potential difference is measured in volts (V).

options.text: The ratio of the power dissipated between the points to the mass of charge.
options.explanation: This statement describes neither electric field nor electric potential difference accurately. Power is the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred, typically measured in watts (W). The concept of power dissipation may relate to energy loss in a circuit, but it doesn't directly define electric potential difference.

options.text: The work done in moving a test charge between points divided by the magnitude of the test charge.
options.explanation: This statement correctly defines electric potential difference. Electric potential difference (V) between two points is the work done per unit charge (Q) in moving a test charge from one point to the other. Mathematically, it is expressed as V=W/Q​, where V is the potential difference, W is the work done, and Q is the magnitude of the test charge.

options.text: The force required to move a unit positive charge between the points per unit charge.
options.explanation: This statement describes electric field strength, not electric potential difference. Electric field strength (E) is the force experienced by a unit positive charge at a point in an electric field. It's measured in newtons per coulomb (N/C). While electric potential difference is related to electric field, they represent different aspects of the electric field.

questions.explanation: This question is simply revising how the electric potential is defined as it is work done per unit charge in moving that charge from one place to the other in the electric field.All other options are not the definitions of electric potential.
hints: describes the electric potential difference between two points in the electric field of charge Q

questions.text: A sphere of charge +Q is fixed in a position. A smaller sphere of charge +q is placed near the larger sphere and released from rest. The small sphere will move away from the large sphere with:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. The velocity cannot decrease because the small sphere is repelled, causing it to accelerate.
This is incorrect. The velocity increases, and the acceleration is not constant due to the changing distance between the charges.

This is correct. The small sphere moves away from the larger one due to repulsion, causing its velocity to increase. However, as it moves further away, the force and thus the acceleration decrease according to the inverse square law.
This is incorrect. Although the velocity increases, the acceleration decreases because the repulsive force weakens with distance.

questions.explanation: When the smaller charge is placed, it undergoes repulsion as ‘like’ charges repel. Since it was at zero velocity, the velocity increases. But as the charge moves further away, the force of repulsion decreases, and according to the formula, F= kQq/r2, acceleration decreases.

questions.text: The values of electric intensity will _ due to the presence of dielectric medium:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that the electric intensity (electric field strength) increases due to the presence of a dielectric medium. When a dielectric material is introduced into an electric field, the polarization of the material occurs, leading to the creation of induced dipoles. These induced dipoles weaken the overall electric field inside the dielectric, which reduces the net electric intensity. Therefore, the electric intensity does not increase; instead, it decreases.

This option implies a rapid and significant increase in electric intensity due to the presence of a dielectric medium. However, the behavior of electric intensity in the presence of a dielectric is not exponential; instead, it decreases gradually and depends on factors such as the dielectric constant of the material and the applied electric field strength.

This option correctly describes the effect of a dielectric medium on electric intensity. When a dielectric material is inserted into an electric field, it reduces the overall electric field strength within the material. This reduction in electric field strength decreases the electric intensity or electric field inside the dielectric medium. The decrease in electric intensity is proportional to the dielectric constant of the material and the strength of the applied electric field.

This option suggests that the electric intensity does not change in the presence of a dielectric medium. However, as explained earlier, the introduction of a dielectric material alters the electric field distribution, leading to a decrease in electric intensity within the dielectric. Therefore, the electric intensity does not remain the same.

questions.explanation: The strength of the electric field is reduced due to the presence of dielectric. If the total charge on the plates is kept constant, then the potential difference is reduced across the capacitor plates.

questions.text: “The magnitude of electrostatic force between two point charges is directly proportional to the product of the magnitude of the charges and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.” This is known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Gauss's Law relates the electric flux passing through a closed surface to the charge enclosed by that surface. It does not directly address the force between point charges.
Ohm's Law describes the relationship between voltage, current, and resistance in an electrical circuit, not the interaction between point charges.
Faraday's Law deals with electromagnetic induction, which is about inducing an electromotive force in a conductor due to a changing magnetic field, not electrostatic forces between charges.
Newton's Laws of motion pertain to the relationship between a body and the forces acting upon it, not the electrostatic force between charges.

Coulomb's Law specifically describes the electrostatic force between two point charges, stating it is directly proportional to the product of their charges and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.

questions.explanation: The statement given is a direct description of Coulomb's Law. This law quantitatively defines the electrostatic force between two point charges as F = k(q1*q2)/r², where F is the force, q1 and q2 are the magnitudes of the charges, r is the distance between the charges, and k is Coulomb's constant. This law is fundamental in understanding electrostatic interactions.Other options explain different physical laws: Gauss's Law pertains to electric fields, Ohm's Law to electrical circuits, Faraday's Law to electromagnetic induction, and Newton's Laws to mechanics. None of these describe the electrostatic force between point charges.

questions.text: The study of electric charges at rest in an electric field is known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electromagnetism is a branch of physics that deals with the study of electric and magnetic phenomena, including their interactions with each other. While electromagnetism encompasses a broader range of topics beyond just electric charges at rest, it includes phenomena such as electromagnetic induction, Maxwell's equations, and electromagnetic waves. Therefore, while electromagnetism includes electrostatics as a subset, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

Electrostatics is the branch of physics concerned with the study of stationary electric charges and the electric fields they create. It focuses on understanding the behavior of electric charges when they are at rest and the interactions between them. Topics in electrostatics include Coulomb's law, electric fields, electric potential, capacitance, and the behavior of conductors and insulators. Therefore, electrostatics is specifically dedicated to the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

Quantum physics, also known as quantum mechanics, is a branch of physics that deals with phenomena at the smallest scales, typically at the level of atoms, molecules, and subatomic particles. While quantum physics encompasses a wide range of topics, including the behavior of particles with electric charge, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field. Instead, it addresses phenomena such as wave-particle duality, quantum superposition, and quantum entanglement.

Magnetism is the branch of physics that deals with the study of magnetic fields, magnetic forces, and magnetic materials. While magnetism is closely related to electricity, particularly through the electromagnetic force described by Maxwell's equations, it is not specifically focused on the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field. Instead, magnetism primarily concerns the behavior of magnetic fields and magnetic materials, including phenomena such as magnetic induction, magnetic dipoles, and magnetic domains.

questions.explanation: Electrostatics is the branch of physics concerned with the study of stationary electric charges and the electric fields they create. It focuses on understanding the behavior of electric charges when they are at rest and the interactions between them. Topics in electrostatics include Coulomb's law, electric fields, electric potential, capacitance, and the behavior of conductors and insulators. Therefore, electrostatics is specifically dedicated to the study of electric charges at rest in an electric field.

questions.text: Three times decrease in the distance between the plates of a parallel plate, capacitor will _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. Capacitance is inversely proportional to the distance between the plates. Reducing the distance increases capacitance.
Incorrect. This is a misunderstanding of the relationship between capacitance and distance. Capacitance does not decrease when plate distance is reduced.
Correct. Since capacitance (C) is inversely proportional to the distance (d) between the plates, reducing the distance to one-third triples the capacitance.
Incorrect. The increase in capacitance is directly proportional to the reduction in distance, which in this case is three times, not six.

questions.explanation: Capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is given by the formula C = ε₀(A/d), where C is the capacitance, ε₀ is the permittivity of free space, A is the area of the plates, and d is the distance between the plates. When the distance d is decreased to one-third, the capacitance C increases by a factor of three. Therefore, a three times decrease in the distance results in a threefold increase in capacitance. Other options are incorrect as they either misinterpret or miscalculate the relationship between capacitance and distance.

questions.text: The electric field strength between a pair of plates is "E". If the separation of the plates is doubled and the potential difference between the plates is increased by a factor of four, the new field strength is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A suggests that the electric field strength remains unchanged. However, this is incorrect. When the separation of the plates is doubled and the potential difference between the plates is increased by a factor of four, the electric field strength is not expected to remain the same. Therefore, option A is incorrect.

Option B correctly indicates that the new electric field strength is twice the original electric field strength. Doubling the separation of the plates while quadrupling the potential difference between them results in an electric field strength that is proportional to 4V/2d=2E. Therefore, option B is the correct choice.

Option C suggests that the new electric field strength is four times the original electric field strength. However, this is not the case. Doubling the separation of the plates and quadrupling the potential difference between them does not result in an electric field strength that is directly proportional to 4V/2d=2E. Therefore, option C is incorrect.

Option D suggests that the new electric field strength is eight times the original electric field strength. However, this is not the case. Doubling the separation of the plates and quadrupling the potential difference between them does not result in an electric field strength that is directly proportional to 4V/2d =2E. Therefore, option D is incorrect.

questions.text: An electron is situated midway between two parallel plates 0.5 cm apart. One of the plates is maintained at a potential of 60 volts above the other. The force on the electron is: (e = -1.6x10-19C)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. This calculation likely involves a mistake in determining the electric field strength.
Incorrect. This value is too low. Ensure you are using the correct formula F = eE and check the electric field calculation.
Correct. The force is calculated using F = eE, where E is derived from the potential difference and the separation of the plates.
Incorrect. This value is higher than expected. Double-check the calculation of the electric field and the resulting force.
Incorrect. This value is significantly higher than the correct answer. Review the formula for the electric field and force.

questions.explanation: To solve this problem, we first calculate the electric field (E) between the parallel plates using the formula E = V/d, where V is the potential difference and d is the distance between the plates. Here, V = 60V and d = 0.5 cm = 0.005 m. Thus, E = 60V/0.005m = 12,000 V/m.Next, we apply the formula for the force on a charge in an electric field: F = eE. The charge of an electron is e = -1.6x10-19 C. Therefore, F = (1.6x10-19 C)(12,000 V/m) = 1.92x10-15 N.Option A is incorrect due to a possible miscalculation of E. Option B underestimates the force, likely from an error in applying the formula. Option D overestimates the force, possibly by using incorrect values. Option E significantly overestimates the force, indicating a misunderstanding of the relationship between V, d, and the resulting force.

questions.text: A particle carrying charge of 2e falls through a potential difference of 3.0 V. Calculate energy acquired by it.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. Using the formula Energy = qV, where q = 2e and V = 3.0 V, we get Energy = 6eV. Converting to joules, Energy = 6 × 1.6 × 10-19 J = 9.6 × 10-19 J.
Incorrect. This value does not match the product of 6eV converted to joules.
Incorrect. This value is not obtained from the correct calculation of charge and potential difference.
Incorrect. This value exceeds the correct energy calculation for the given variables.
Incorrect. This value is not calculated from the formula Energy = qV with the given parameters.

questions.explanation: To find the energy acquired by the particle, use the formula: Energy = qV, where q is the charge and V is the potential difference. The charge of the particle is 2e, where e is the elementary charge, approximately 1.6 × 10-19 C. The potential difference is given as 3.0 V. Thus, Energy = 2e × 3 V = 6eV. Converting eV to joules gives: Energy = 6 × 1.6 × 10-19 J = 9.6 × 10-19 J.Options B, C, D, and E are incorrect because they do not follow the correct calculation using the formula and given values.

questions.text: The net charge flowing through any section of the conductor per unit time is known as?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electric intensity, or electric field strength, measures the force per unit charge at a point in the field. It is not related to the flow of charge through a conductor.
Electric flux quantifies the number of electric field lines passing through a given area. It does not describe charge flow through a conductor.
Electric current is defined as the net charge flowing through a conductor per unit time. It is effectively the rate of flow of electric charge and is measured in amperes.
Electric potential refers to the potential energy per unit charge at a point in an electric field. It does not describe a flow of charge.
The dielectric constant is a measure of a material's ability to store electrical energy in an electric field. It is unrelated to the concept of charge flow over time.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Electric current. Electric current is the rate at which electric charge flows past a point or through a region. Electric flux deals with the field lines through an area, not the charge flow through a conductor. Electric potential is the work needed to move a charge in an electric field and does not measure flow. Dielectric constant relates to a material's ability to transmit electric fields, not to charge flow.

questions.text: How many electrons should be removed from a coin of mass 1.6 g, so that it may float in an electric field intensity of 109 N/C which is directed upwards?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is correct because it correctly calculates the number of electrons that need to be removed to balance the gravitational force with the electric force.
This option is incorrect. It underestimates the number of electrons needed to create an electric force equal to the gravitational force, leading to an incorrect balance.
This option is incorrect. It significantly underestimates the number of electrons, resulting in an insufficient electric force to counteract gravity.
This option is incorrect. It dramatically underestimates the number of electrons, failing to provide enough electric force to make the coin float.

questions.explanation: To make the coin float, the upward electric force must equal the downward gravitational force. The gravitational force can be calculated using Fgravity = mg, where m = 1.6 g = 0.0016 kg and g = 9.8 m/s2. The electric force is Felectric = QE, where Q is the charge and E = 109 N/C. Setting Fgravity equal to Felectric, we have mg = QE. Solving for Q gives Q = (mg)/E = (0.0016 kg × 9.8 m/s2) / (109 N/C) = 1.568 × 10-8 C. The charge of an electron is approximately 1.6 × 10-19 C, so the number of electrons is Q/e = (1.568 × 10-8 C) / (1.6 × 10-19 C/electron) ≈ 9.8 × 107 electrons. Therefore, the correct answer is 9.8 × 107. The other options incorrectly calculate the number of electrons, leading to an imbalance of forces.

questions.text: Two bodies are charged by rubbing one against the other. During the process, one becomes positively charged while the other becomes negatively charged. Then:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Mass of each body remains unchanged
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the transfer of electrons actually causes a slight change in the mass of each body.

options.text: Mass of each body changes marginally
options.explanation: While this option acknowledges a change in mass, it does not address the conservation of total mass in the system.

options.text: Mass of each body changes slightly and hence the total mass
options.explanation: This option is incorrect as it contradicts the law of conservation of mass, which states that the total mass should remain constant.

options.text: Mass of each body changes slightly but the total mass remains the same
options.explanation: This is the correct answer. The transfer of electrons between the bodies results in a slight change in their individual masses, but the total mass of the system remains unchanged due to the conservation of mass.

questions.explanation: When two bodies are charged by rubbing, electrons are transferred from one body to the other. This leads to one body becoming negatively charged (gaining electrons) and the other positively charged (losing electrons). In this process, the mass of the negatively charged body slightly increases, while the mass of the positively charged body slightly decreases. However, according to the law of conservation of mass, the total mass of the system remains the same because the electrons are merely transferred from one body to the other, not created or destroyed.Option A is incorrect because it overlooks the mass change due to electron transfer. Option B notes a mass change but fails to recognize that the total mass remains constant. Option C incorrectly suggests that the total mass changes, which violates the conservation of mass. Option D correctly identifies that individual masses change while the total mass remains constant.

questions.text: A neutron, a proton, and an electron are placed in a uniform electric field.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The forces acting on them will be equal
options.explanation: Forces in an electric field depend on the charge of the particle. Since only the proton and electron are charged, while the neutron is not, the forces cannot be equal. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
Acceleration is determined by both the force and the mass of the particle (a = F/m). Since the forces and masses are different, their accelerations cannot be equal. Thus, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Magnitude of acceleration may be equal
options.explanation: The forces on the proton and electron are of equal magnitude but opposite direction, and combined with their different masses, their accelerations will differ in magnitude. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Magnitude of acceleration will be different
options.explanation: The proton and electron experience forces of equal magnitude but opposite direction, and their differing masses result in different accelerations. Therefore, the magnitude of their accelerations will be different. This option is correct.

questions.explanation: In a uniform electric field, the neutron experiences no force because it is uncharged. The proton experiences a force of qE and the electron experiences a force of -qE. These forces have the same magnitude but opposite directions due to the opposite charges. The mass of the proton is much greater than that of the electron, leading to different accelerations as calculated by a = F/m. Thus, the magnitude of the acceleration will differ between these particles, confirming that Option D is correct. Options A, B, and C are incorrect because they either assume equal forces or accelerations, which contradicts the principles of electrostatics and the relationship between force, mass, and acceleration.

questions.text: A hollow charged metal sphere has radius r. If the potential difference between its surface and a point at a distance 3r from the centre is V, then electric field intensity at distance 3r from the centre is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option incorrectly assumes a direct relationship without considering the full electrostatic balance. For a point at 3r, the uniform field outside should be considered, leading to V/6r.
This choice doesn't apply the correct formula for calculating electric field intensity from potential difference at the specified distance. The correct relationship is V/6r, not V/4r.
This is the correct answer. For a hollow charged sphere, the electric field intensity at a distance 3r from the center is V divided by the effective distance 3r, resulting in V/6r.
This option overestimates the field intensity by not accounting for the total distance from the center. The correct calculation uses the full distance (3r), resulting in V/6r, not V/2r.

questions.explanation: For a hollow charged metal sphere, the electric field intensity outside the sphere is determined by the potential difference V and the radial distance from the center. The potential difference V between the sphere's surface and a point at distance 3r is calculated using the formula for electric field intensity E = V/d, where d = 3r. Therefore, the correct electric field intensity is E = V/6r. Option C correctly identifies this relationship. Options A, B, and D provide incorrect calculations either by misapplying the formula or using incorrect distances.

questions.text: 1 volt x 1 ampere is equal to:

options.explanation: 1 coulomb represents the amount of charge, not the power. The relationship between voltage, current, and power is not directly defined by charge alone.
1 newton is a unit of force. Although it is a fundamental unit in physics, it does not relate to the concepts of voltage, current, or power.
1 watt is the unit of power in the International System of Units (SI), and is defined as 1 volt multiplied by 1 ampere. Therefore, this is the correct answer.
1 horsepower (hp) is a unit of power that is equivalent to about 746 watts. While it is a unit of power, it is not equivalent to 1 volt x 1 ampere.

questions.explanation: According to the formula for electrical power, Power (P) = Voltage (V) x Current (I). Hence, when you multiply 1 volt by 1 ampere, you get 1 watt. That's why option C is the correct answer. Option A (1 coulomb) is incorrect because coulombs measure electric charge, not power. Option B (1 newton) is also incorrect as newtons measure force and do not relate to electrical power. Lastly, Option D (1 hp) is incorrect because while it is a unit of power, it is much larger than 1 watt and does not represent the correct value derived from the given voltage and current.

questions.text: Ampere.second is the unit of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Power is measured in watts (W), which is defined as joules per second (J/s). This is not related to the unit of charge.
The unit of charge is the coulomb (C), which is defined as the quantity of electricity transported in one second by a current of one ampere. Therefore, ampere.second is indeed a unit of charge.
Potential difference is measured in volts (V), which is defined as joules per coulomb (J/C). This does not correspond to ampere.second.
Current is measured in amperes (A). While the unit ampere involves the concept of charge, ampere.second specifically refers to the amount of charge, not current itself.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Charge because the unit ampere.second represents a coulomb, which is the standard unit of electric charge. According to the definition of a coulomb, it is the amount of electric charge transported by a constant current of one ampere in one second. In contrast, the options for Power (watts), Potential Difference (volts), and Current (amperes) are all different physical quantities with their own distinct units. Power relates to energy transfer, potential difference measures energy per charge, and current measures the flow of electric charge per unit time, but none of these relate directly to the unit of charge itself.

questions.text: Two equal and opposite charges of 10 C are separated at a distance of 10 cm, the electric potential at mid-point between the charges is:

options.explanation: This option suggests that the potential is the sum of the potentials from both charges, which isn't correct since they cancel each other out at the midpoint.

This option assumes a contribution from one charge without considering the opposing influence of the second charge at the midpoint. The potentials from equal and opposite charges cancel each other out.
This option implies a partial contribution from one charge, but again neglects the cancellation effect of the opposite charge at the midpoint.
This is the correct answer because at the midpoint between two equal and opposite charges, the potentials due to each charge cancel each other out, resulting in a net potential of 0 V.

questions.explanation: At the midpoint between two equal and opposite charges, the electric potential contributions from each charge are equal in magnitude but opposite in sign. Therefore, they cancel each other out, resulting in a total electric potential of 0 V at that point. This is why the correct answer is 0 V. The other options suggest a non-zero potential, which does not consider the crucial fact that the charges are equal and opposite, leading to a complete cancellation of their effects at the midpoint.

questions.text: The force of repulsion between two alike charges is 10 N in vacuum. When a material of Ep= 2 is placed between them. New force will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests an increase in force, which is incorrect as the dielectric material reduces the force between the charges.
This option implies a minor reduction in force, but does not account for the full effect of the dielectric polarization factor.
This option indicates no change in force, which is incorrect as the presence of the dielectric will reduce the force.
This is the correct answer. The force of repulsion is reduced by the factor of the dielectric constant, Ep, resulting in a new force of 10 N / 2 = 5 N.

questions.explanation: In this problem, we start with a repulsive force of 10 N between two like charges in a vacuum. When a dielectric material with an electric polarization factor of Ep= 2 is introduced, the force between the charges is reduced by this factor. The relationship governing this change is given by:F' = F / Ep,where F' is the new force, F is the original force (10 N), and Ep is the electric polarization factor (which is 2 in this case). Thus:F' = 10 N / 2 = 5 N.This shows that the force is halved due to the presence of the dielectric. The other options are incorrect as they either suggest an increase in force or no change at all, which contradicts the effect of the dielectric material on the electric forces between the charges.

questions.text: The slope-of the charge-time graph for a charging capacitor gives:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The slope of the charge-time graph represents the rate at which charge accumulates on the capacitor. This rate is defined as current, which is the amount of charge per unit time.

Voltage is related to the charge stored in the capacitor, but does not directly represent the slope of the charge-time graph. It reflects the potential difference across the capacitor rather than the rate of charge accumulation.
Force is not relevant in this context, as the question specifically pertains to charge and time. Force is not a measure that can be derived from the charge-time graph.

Energy relates to the amount of work done to charge the capacitor, but it is not represented by the slope of the charge-time graph. The graph specifically indicates the rate of charge flow, which is current.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Current, as the slope of the charge-time graph indicates how quickly charge is flowing into the capacitor, which is defined as current (I = dq/dt). A steeper slope corresponds to a higher current, meaning more charge is accumulating over time. The other options do not represent this relationship: Voltage is related to the charge stored but not the rate of charge flow, Force is unrelated to charge and time, and Energy pertains to the work done to charge the capacitor rather than the rate of charge accumulation.

questions.text: Two small charged objects attract each other with a force F when separated by a distance d. If the charge on each object is reduced to one-fourth of its original value and the distance between them is reduced to d/2 the force becomes:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because reducing the charge to one-fourth and the distance to d/2 does not result in such a decrease in force. The calculation shows that the force will not be this low.
This option is also incorrect. While it accounts for the reduction in charge, it underestimates the effect of halving the distance, which increases the force significantly.
This option is correct. By reducing the charge to one-fourth and halving the distance, the force becomes F/4, as calculated using Coulomb's Law.
This option is incorrect. While it considers the change in distance, it fails to fully account for the reduction in charge, resulting in a force that is higher than it should be.

questions.explanation: According to Coulomb's Law, the force F between two point charges is given by the formula: F = k * (q1 * q2) / d^2, where k is the electrostatic constant, q1 and q2 are the magnitudes of the charges, and d is the distance between them. In this scenario, the charges are reduced to one-fourth of their original values, so we can write this as:F' = k * ((q1/4) * (q2/4)) / (d/2)^2Now simplifying this gives:F' = k * (q1 * q2) / (16 * (d^2/4)) = k * (q1 * q2) / (4 * d^2)This means that the new force F' is F/4. Therefore, option C is correct. The other options miscalculate the force due to incorrect consideration of both charge reduction and distance reduction.

questions.text: If both the plate area and the plate separation of a parallel-plate capacitor doubled, the capacitance is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that capacitance increases with the area increase while not considering the effect of increased separation. However, both changes affect capacitance.
This option implies that the capacitance would decrease due to increased separation, but it neglects the fact that the area has also doubled.
This is the correct answer because while the area is doubled, the separation is also doubled, resulting in no net change in capacitance according to the formula.
This option incorrectly suggests a threefold increase in capacitance, which overlooks the balancing effect of increasing both area and separation.

questions.explanation: The capacitance (C) of a parallel-plate capacitor is given by the formula C = (ε₀ * A) / d, where A is the area of the plates, d is the separation between the plates, and ε₀ is the permittivity of free space. When both the plate area (A) and the plate separation (d) are doubled, the new capacitance becomes C' = (ε₀ * (2A)) / (2d) = (ε₀ * A) / d = C. Thus, the capacitance remains unchanged.Option A is incorrect as it mistakenly assumes that only the area increase affects capacitance without accounting for the separation change. Option B incorrectly assumes that the increase in separation alone would reduce capacitance. Option D miscalculates the relationship entirely by suggesting an increase by a factor of three.

questions.text: A 20𝜇F capacitor is charged 200 V. Its stored energy is:

questions.explanation: The energy stored in a capacitor can be calculated using the formula E = 0.5 * C * V^2. Here, C is the capacitance in farads, and V is the voltage. Given that C = 20μF (which is 20 × 10^-6 F) and V = 200 V, we can substitute these values into the formula:E = 0.5 * (20 × 10^-6) * (200^2) = 0.5 * (20 × 10^-6) * 40000 = 0.5 * 0.8 = 0.4 J.Thus, the correct answer is 0.4 J. The other options are incorrect because they either vastly overestimate or underestimate the energy based on the formula and the given values.

questions.text: The bird perching on a high-power line does not get electroshock. This is because.

options.text: The whole body of the bird sitting on the live wire is at the same potential and hence no current flows through the body

questions.text: The coulomb's constant k depends upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: nature of medium and system of units
options.explanation: Coulomb's constant, denoted by k, is a fundamental constant in electrostatics that determine the strength of the electrostatic force between two charged particles. It is expressed as: k = 1 / (4πεo) Where εo is the permittivity of free space. The value of k depends on both the nature of the medium and the system of units being used. The nature of the medium affects the permittivity, which is a measure of how easily electric fields can propagate through a material. Similarly, the system of units used can also affect the value of Coulomb's constant.

questions.text: A charged particle is moving in a uniform electric field. For the motion of the particle due to the field, which quantity has a constant non-zero value?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In the case of a charged particle moving in a uniform electric field, the acceleration of the particle will have a constant non-zero value. This is because the electric field exerts a constant force on the charged particle, causing it to accelerate continuously in the direction of the electric field.
The displacement of the particle will not have a constant non-zero value. It will depend on the initial conditions, the time elapsed, and the acceleration of the particle.
The rate of change of acceleration will not have a constant non-zero value. It can vary depending on the motion of the charged particle and the applied electric field.

The velocity of the particle will not have a constant non-zero value. Initially, the particle may have an initial velocity, but as it accelerates under the influence of the electric field, the velocity will change continuously.

questions.explanation: In the case of a charged particle moving in a uniform electric field, the acceleration of the particle will have a constant non-zero value. This is because the electric field exerts a constant force on the charged particle, causing it to accelerate continuously in the direction of the electric field.

questions.text: Electric intensity due to charge distribution is calculated using which of the following?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Ohm's Law relates the current flowing through a conductor to the voltage applied across it and the resistance of the conductor. It applies to direct current (DC) circuits and is not directly related to the calculation of electric field intensity due to charge distribution.

Faraday's law describes the relationship between a changing magnetic field and the induced electromotive force in a conductor. It is used to calculate electromagnetic induction and is not directly related to the electric intensity due to charge distributions.

Gauss's law is one of the fundamental equations of electromagnetism. It relates the electric flux through a closed surface to the charge enclosed within that surface. Gauss's law allows us to calculate the electric field or electric intensity due to a given charge distribution.

Ampere's Law relates the magnetic field created by a current-carrying wire or current distribution to the current enclosed by a closed loop. It is used to calculate the magnetic field, not the electric field, around current-carrying conductors.

questions.explanation: Gauss's law allows us to calculate the electric field or electric intensity due to a given charge distribution.

questions.text: The capacitance of a capacitor does not depend upon
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The capacitance of a capacitor is directly proportional to the area of the plates. Increasing the plate area increases the capacitance because there is more surface area available for charge accumulation.

The capacitance of a capacitor does depend on the medium between the plates. The dielectric constant or relative permittivity of the medium affects the capacitance. Different dielectric materials have different abilities to store electric charge, leading to different capacitance values.

The capacitance of a capacitor is inversely proportional to the distance between the plates. Decreasing the distance between the plates increases the capacitance because the electric field lines between the plates become stronger, allowing for more charge to accumulate.

The thickness of the plates does not affect the capacitance. The capacitance depends on the area of the plates, but not on their thickness. The thickness only affects other properties such as the mechanical strength or resistance to heat, but not the ability to store electric charge.

questions.explanation: The thickness of the plates does not affect the capacitance. The capacitance depends on the area of the plates, but not on their thickness. The thickness only affects other properties such as the mechanical strength or resistance to heat, but not the ability to store electric charge.

questions.text: The electric potential at the center of uniformly charged conducting sphere is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is not correct.

options.text: Less than that at the surface
options.explanation: This option is not correct.

options.text: Greater than that at the surface
options.explanation: This option is not correct.

options.text: Equal to that at the surface
options.explanation: This is the correct option. Potential difference is constant inside the charged sphere as charges are uniformly distributed on the surface.

questions.explanation: According to Gauss's law, the electric field inside a uniformly charged conducting sphere is zero. Therefore, the electric potential at any point inside the sphere, including the centre, is equal to the electric potential at the surface. This is because in a conductor, charges redistribute themselves on the surface to achieve electrostatic equilibrium. As a result, the excess charge resides on the outer surface of the conductor, causing equipotential surfaces to form. Since the electric potential is the same at all points on an equipotential surface, the electric potential at the center of the conducting sphere is equal to that at the surface.
